hotspots.title: Pete Hegseth’s Pentagon purges and Iran tensions
hotspots.blurb: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s aggressive military leadership reshuffle, including the firing of Navy Secretary John Phelan, has dominated coverage amid escalating U.S.-Iran tensions and a global naval blockade. The purge is framed as a decisive but polarizing move to assert control over the Pentagon.
hotspots.affect: This commands attention due to its high-stakes geopolitical implications and the moral panic over perceived military overreach, with critics decrying it as a politicization of the armed forces.

hotspots.title: Vox’s ‘national priority’ surge in Spain
hotspots.blurb: Vox’s ‘national priority’ policy, which prioritizes Spanish citizens in public services and welfare, has become a defining feature of its coalition deals with the PP in Extremadura and Aragón. The policy is sparking legal and ethical debates over discrimination and xenophobia.
hotspots.affect: This hotspot is charged due to its moral panic over immigration and welfare, with critics framing it as a racist policy while supporters defend it as common-sense governance.

hotspots.title: Hungary’s Tisza Party landslide and reforms
hotspots.blurb: Péter Magyar’s Tisza Party secured a historic two-thirds majority in Hungary’s April elections, ending Viktor Orbán’s 16-year rule. The new government is pushing sweeping reforms, including opening secret police archives and revising economic policies, amid high expectations and scrutiny.
hotspots.affect: This commands attention due to the dramatic shift in power, the moral charge of anti-corruption reforms, and the geopolitical implications of Hungary’s pivot away from Russia.

hotspots.title: Reform UK’s ‘patriotic’ school agenda
hotspots.blurb: Reform UK’s proposal to mandate Union Jack displays, King’s portraits, and a ‘patriotic’ British history curriculum in schools has sparked intense debate. The policy is framed as either a restoration of national pride or a nationalist overreach.
hotspots.affect: This hotspot is charged due to its moral panic over education and national identity, with critics accusing Reform UK of promoting propaganda while supporters praise it as a return to traditional values.

hotspots.title: AfD’s rise and democratic backlash in Germany
hotspots.blurb: Germany’s far-right AfD party is surging in polls, prompting other parties to enact parliamentary reforms to limit its influence. The AfD’s radical rhetoric and ties to extremism have sparked fears of democratic erosion, with critics calling for a party ban.
hotspots.affect: This commands attention due to the moral panic over far-right extremism and the polarized debate over how to counter the AfD’s rise without normalizing its policies.
